message:
fix(messaging): add Finalizing transaction phase, GC obsolete WAL segments, simplify consumer poll pinning

Kafka transactional producer (kafka.rs):
- Replace the (active: bool, needs_abort: bool) pair with a
  TransactionPhase enum: Idle, Active, Finalizing, NeedsAbortRecovery.
  The previous two-bool representation had an impossible state
  (active=true, needs_abort=true) and no way to distinguish "commit/
  abort RPC in flight" from "transaction open for sends". The Finalizing
  phase blocks new begin_transaction and send calls while the broker-
  side commit/abort completes, preventing a race where a concurrent
  begin could observe Idle before the broker acknowledges the previous
  transaction's outcome.
- mark_transaction_dropped now only poisons Active and Finalizing
  phases (not Idle or already-NeedsAbortRecovery), preventing a
  double-drop from clearing a legitimately idle state.
- activate_transaction, ensure_active_transaction, mark_transaction_
  finalizing, mark_transaction_idle, mark_transaction_needs_abort:
  new methods replacing the old ensure_ready_to_begin +
  mark_transaction_finished, with explicit match arms for each phase.
- Added tests: rejects begin while Finalizing, rejects send while
  Finalizing, drop poisons Active and Finalizing, drop in Finalizing
  requires abort recovery.

Kafka consumer (kafka_consumer.rs):
- Replace futures_lite::pin!(state_wait) / pin!(broker_wait) with
  mutable let bindings and Pin::new(&mut ...) in poll_fn closures.
  The pin! macro moves the value into a shadowed Pin<&mut _> binding,
  but the closure captured the pre-move reference, causing a borrow-
  after-move when the Notified future was polled. Using mutable
  bindings with Pin::new avoids the move entirely.

WAL storage (stream.rs):
- Add garbage_collect_segments(): after truncate_through removes
  entries, segments whose last_seq ≤ through_seq are now deleted
  from disk. Previously obsolete segment files accumulated forever,
  wasting disk space proportional to stream lifetime. The method
  preserves at least one segment (the active one) and removes files
  synchronously during the truncation call.
- Updated test assertion: segment_count after truncation is now 2
  (not 4) because the two fully-consumed segments are cleaned up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
